The bell rang twice from the harbor tower, then stopped halfway through what should have been the third stroke. Everyone in the room looked up, not because the bell was loud, but because unfinished sounds make a sharper claim on attention than complete ones.

Mira closed the ledger, waited a beat, and said, "Begin again from the marked line."

[Section break]

When the reading resumed, the voice entered on the word "however," which made the paragraph sound as if it had been arguing in private before anyone arrived to listen. The sentence was not wrong, exactly, but it carried the faint seam of a restart: too upright at the beginning, too polished in the first breath, as though it needed three clauses to remember it had once been moving.

[Paragraph break]

Later, after a page turn and a pause long enough for a chair to creak, Tomas tried once more. This time he began with the final phrase of the previous paragraph, letting the cadence spill forward instead of standing up from a full stop. The effect was small but undeniable. What had sounded assembled now sounded continued.
